mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and overall economic health. This strong spending environment directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the short term.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending supports equity markets
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies broader economic contractions. This weakening economic outlook leads to reduced corporate profitability and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such events, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation, coupled with an active Federal Reserve tightening policy (indicated by rising federal funds rate), increases the cost of capital and reduces the present value of future corporate earnings. This combination leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 5% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in 3 months
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL is above 5% and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% over 3 months, yet S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of these instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts during an early economic expansion indicate strong underlying demand, investment, and consumer confidence. This translates into broader economic growth, increased construction activity, and positive equity market performance over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a leading indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent growth during an early-cycle expansion, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strong manufacturing activity and supply-side expansion, suggesting healthy corporate output and profitability. This positive signal for industrial sector performance drives short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO monthly growth exceeds 0.5%, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls reflects a robust labor market and an expanding economy. This strong employment environment supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive growth, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant or contracting GDP growth combined with negative retail sales growth indicates a significant weakening in aggregate demand. This signals an impending or ongoing economic contraction that will negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1% YoY AND RSAFS growth < 0% MoM
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is below 1% YoY and RSAFS growth is negative MoM, yet S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of such periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces borrowing costs for companies and encourages investment. This supportive financial environment typically underpins positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations during such periods, this hypothesis is falsified.
